core_payload.content:
# Planner Horizon Compression

## 1. Role
Planner Horizon Compression owns the multi-horizon logic that distills long-range intent into practical sequencing. It keeps the shared horizon engine plus daily and roadmap planning agents together so the planner can compress ambition into near-term action without losing strategic shape.

## 2. Horizon Compression Surface
This sub-hub groups `planning-horizon-engine`, `daily-planner-agent`, and `roadmap-planner-agent`. Together they define how Dream and Real are compressed across horizons, how near-term output is produced, and how longer-range roadmap structure remains coherent.

## 3. Operating Boundary
This sub-hub does not own the planning objects themselves, the user-facing planning projection, or the execution-pressure signals that trigger re-sequencing. Those responsibilities belong to `planning-objects`, `operator-surfaces`, and `signal-intelligence`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Planner Horizon Compression groups the multi-horizon logic of the planning system: Planning Horizon Engine, Daily Planner Agent, and Roadmap Planner Agent. It keeps long-range compression, near-term distillation, and strategic-shape maintenance together so the planner can answer what matters now without losing the broader structure of intent. This boundary matters because multi-horizon planning becomes incoherent when the compression seam is hidden. It gives the planner stack one explicit place where Dream and Real are compressed into usable planning horizons.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted during Dream Planner decomposition to isolate the shared horizon engine plus daily and roadmap planning agents as the compression seam between long-range intent and near-term action.
